bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just beginning (t=0.0) and I am making the first bid, I will start by proposing the outcome that maximizes my utility. My preferred value across all issues is 'valueC', so I will request this to establish a strong opening position.

The negotiation is in its very early stages, and the opponent has proposed an offer at the literal opposite end of my preference spectrum (suggesting they favor valueA across the board). To signal a willingness to negotiate while preserving near-maximum utility, I will make a minor concession on one of my least important issues (issueA) by moving it to valueB, keeping the most critical issues at valueC.

The negotiation is very early at t=0.04, and the opponent has repeated their opening offer of minimal utility (all valueA). To signal willingness to negotiate without significantly sacrificing overall utility, I will make a small concession on my lowest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB), while holding firm on the higher-weighted issues (issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF).

The opponent is repeatedly anchoring on their opening offer of all 'valueA'. Since the time fraction is still extremely early (t=0.06), I need to gently explore their preferences without sacrificing much utility. I will fully concede on one of my least important issues (issueA) to see if giving them their preferred value on this issue prompts any reciprocal concession, while maintaining my most important issues at their maximum utility values.

The time fraction is still very early (t=0.08), but the opponent has remained completely unyielding, demanding 'valueA' across all issues in every turn. I will continue to make small, measured concessions on my lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) to encourage reciprocity, while holding firm on my highest-weight issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, and issueD) where I receive my maximum utility.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their extreme opening offer, consistently bidding 'valueA' for all issues despite my concessions. At t=0.10, which is still very early in the negotiation, I will fully concede on my lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB to valueA) to demonstrate flexibility and see if this prompts any reciprocation. I will firmly hold my highest-weight issues (issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF) at valueC to protect my core utility, maintaining an overall target utility of 0.84.

It is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.12), and the opponent has stubbornly repeated their opening offer of 'valueA' for all issues without making any concessions. I have already conceded fully on my two lowest-priority issues (issueA and issueB). To show a willingness to negotiate while protecting my core interests, I will make a minor concession on issueC from valueC to valueB, reducing my overall utility slightly but maintaining firm positions on my highest-priority issues (issueD, issueE, and issueF).

The time fraction is still very early (t=0.14), but the opponent has continually repeated their opening offer of all `valueA`s, showing no willingness to concede. To test which issues the opponent values most while holding my total utility around 0.86, I will rotate my concessions. I have already capitulated on the low-weight issues A and B. Now I will return issue C to `valueC` and instead concede issue D to `valueB`, protecting my most important issues (F and E).

The negotiation is still early (t=0.16), but the opponent has held firm on their initial offer without making any concessions, despite my significant moves on the lower-weight issues (issueA and issueB). Since the opponent's repeated offer yields a very low utility for me, I will maintain my current utility level (around 0.86) by shifting my middle-weight concession from issueD to issueE. This allows me to explore the opponent's preferences without conceding further value, while strictly protecting my high-priority issueF.

The opponent has stubbornly maintained the exact same offer since the beginning of the negotiation, demanding `valueA` for all issues. Since it is still early (t=0.18), I will not concede aggressively but will continue exploring combinations to see if giving ground on my less critical issues (like issueB and issueA) or mid-importance issues (like issueE) triggers any reciprocity. I am maintaining my most critical issues, like issueF, at my most desired value.

The opponent is holding strictly firm on their opening offer of all 'valueA' across every single bid, showing no willingness to concede at this early stage. I will construct a bid that maintains my top utility on my most critical issues (issueF, issueC, issueE) while exploring partial concessions to 'valueB' on issueD and 'valueA' on issueA to gauge if a slight shift triggers a response. I will continue to protect my core utility while applying patient time pressure.

The time fraction is still low (0.22), giving enough room to be patient as the opponent hasn't conceded from their opening offer of all 'valueA's. I am still keeping our highest-weight issue (F) and several other high-weight issues at their maximum utility for us (valueC). I'll offer a minor concession on issueC and issueA to probe for flexibility while keeping expected utility above 0.89 (relative to a 1.1 max).

The opponent is completely holding firm, repeatedly offering 'valueA' for every issue, which yields my reservation utility (0.2). Since the time fraction is still fairly early (t=0.24), there is no need to make large concessions yet. I will maintain a high-utility target around 0.94 by conceding slightly on my lower-weight issues (issueA and issueC) while holding firm on heavily weighted issues, especially issueF, to search for any willingness to compromise.

The opponent continues to repeat their opening offer consisting exclusively of `valueA`, showing no willingness to concede at this stage. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.26), I will hold firm on my highest-priority issue (`issueF`) and maintain a high overall utility while testing alternative concessions on the lower-weight issues. I will offer `valueB` on `issueD` and `valueA` on `issueB` to search for a tradeoff that might elicit a counterpart concession.

The opponent is using a fixed-offer strategy, continually proposing the lowest possible utility across all issues (valueA for everything). Given that we are still in the early phase of the negotiation (t=0.28), I will maintain high values on my most important issues (issueF, issueC, issueE) while exploring slightly different combinations on lower-weight attributes like issueA, issueB, and issueD. This tests whether the opponent is willing to move off their extreme anchor to accept partial concessions.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of all `valueA`s without proposing any concessions or providing any messages. Since we are at `t=0.3`, I do not need to rush a massive concession but should start experimenting with slightly lower utilities. I will concede partially on `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC` while strictly protecting my highest-weight issues (`issueF`, `issueE`, `issueD`) to see if I can draw the opponent out of their hardline stance.

The opponent continues to hold firm on their opening bid proposing `valueA` across all issues, showing no signs of compromise. In response to their rigidity and because the time fraction is still fairly early (t=0.32), I will concede entirely to `valueA` on my lowest utility issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) to directly cater to their demands, while making moderate concessions (`valueB`) on some mid-tier issues (`issueC` and `issueE`). However, I will strongly hold firm on `issueF` and `issueD` to maintain a viable utility for myself.

The opponent is persistently holding firm on their opening offer of all `valueA`s, showing no concessions so far. Since the time fraction is only 0.34, I have room to steadily explore tradeoffs without drastically decreasing my utility. I am keeping my most important issue (issueF) at maximum utility while proposing a mix of `valueA` and `valueB` on lower- and middle-weight issues (issueA, issueB, issueC, issueD) to find what might appeal to the opponent without dropping my utility below an acceptable mid-game range.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on `valueA` for all issues without making any concessions. Since it is still early-mid game (t=0.36), I will make a significant concession by dropping another issue to their preferred `valueA` (conceding completely on my lower-weighted issues A, B, and C) while firmly holding my highest-weighted issues D, E, and F at my top preference `valueC`. This maintains a high utility of 0.78 for me and tests if the opponent is willing to accept a partial compromise.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, consistently offering `valueA` for everything since the beginning. I need to maintain a moderately high utility while continuing to seek an opening. I will propose an offer that gives the opponent their preferred `valueA` on my low-weight issue `issueA` and another high-weight issue `issueC`, while remaining firm on the critical issues `issueE` and `issueF` to preserve my own utility at around 0.82.

At t=0.40, the opponent has stubbornly repeated their initial opening bid across all periods. To coax them into moving, I will continue systematically probing for a compromise. In this turn, I will yield completely on issueD while staying firm on my highest-importance issue (issueF) and my remaining high-weight issues (issueC, issueE) in order to maintain a strong overall utility.

The opponent has consistently offered `valueA` for every single issue without fail. Since we are approaching the middle of the negotiation (t=0.42) and the opponent is holding completely firm, I will continue to signal flexibility by conceding fully to `valueA` on two low-importance issues (`issueB` and `issueC`), while holding steadfast on my highest value issues (`issueD`, `issueE`, and `issueF`), keeping my utility slightly above 0.80. This maintains a strong stance while offering concessions where they matter least to me.

The opponent is employing a strict hard-bargaining strategy, repeatedly proposing 'valueA' for all issues without any concessions or messages. As the time fraction is approaching 0.45, I will make a moderate shift by granting 'valueA' on my low-priority issues (A and B), and partially conceding with 'valueB' on my medium-priority issues (D and E). However, I will hold firm on 'valueC' for my highest-priority issues (F and C) to maintain a strong overall utility around 0.78.

The opponent has stubbornly refused to move from their initial offer of "valueA" for all issues across 24 rounds. Approaching the halfway mark of the negotiation, I will probe their priorities by conceding entirely on my lowest priority issues (issueA, issueB) and one mid-priority issue (issueE) to their preferred "valueA". I will maintain my highest priority issue (issueF) and two mid-priority issues (issueC, issueD) at "valueC" to keep my utility reasonable.

The opponent is holding entirely firm across all issues, maintaining their exact opening bid continuously without a single concession. At t=0.48, reaching the middle of the negotiation, I will shift my strategy to offer a broader compromise by setting most of the lower and medium-weighted issues to `valueB`, while holding firm on my most critical priority (`issueF`) at `valueC`. This keeps my expected utility at an acceptable level (around 0.78) while signaling flexibility across the board.

The opponent has rigidly requested `valueA` for all issues without making a single concession so far. Now that we are halfway through the negotiation (`t=0.5`), I am conceding entirely to their preferred `valueA` on my lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) to encourage reciprocity. I will also offer the compromise `valueB` on middle-weight issues, while continuing to hold firm on my highest-priority issue (issueF) to ensure a reasonable utility.

The opponent has rigidly requested `valueA` across all issues for the entire negotiation so far. Since we are around the mid-point (t=0.52), I will further concede on `issueD` to `valueA` to signal continued flexibility, while holding firm on heavily weighted issues, particularly `issueF` and `issueE`. My target utility for this turn drops to roughly 0.70 to test if the opponent will finally respond to concessions.

The opponent has stubbornly insisted on `valueA` for all issues and has not shown any willingness to concede. As the deadline approaches, I will test their boundaries by conceding fully to `valueA` on our lowest weight issues (`issueA`, `issueB`) and one medium issue (`issueC`), and partially conceding to `valueB` on `issueD` and `issueE`. I am still strongly protecting `issueF` at `valueC` because it carries the most weight for my utility.

The opponent is holding extremely firm to their opening offer of 'valueA' for all issues, making no concessions so far. We are in the mid-game (t=0.56) and I need to continue exploring tradeoffs to see if the opponent is willing to accept agreements that fully concede on several issues. I will offer 'valueA' for my lowest-weight issues (issueA, issueB) and one moderately-weighted issue (issueC), while maintaining my highest-weight issue (issueF) at maximum utility and slightly conceding on issueE to keep my overall utility above 0.7.

The opponent is completely unyielding, repeating the exact same bid of `valueA` for all issues across every turn. Since we are just past the midpoint (t=0.58), I will continue to concede on my lowest-priority issues (A and B) and rotate offering their preferred `valueA` on one of my medium-weight issues (D this time). I will firmly hold my highest-value issue (F) at `valueC` to keep my utility around 0.70 while searching for a breakthrough.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, repeatedly demanding `valueA` across the board without making any concessions. As we cross the midpoint (t=0.6), I'm continuing my strategy of conceding on my lower-weight issues to appeal to them, while still preserving utility above 0.70. I will offer `valueA` on issues A, B, and C, but hold my ground on my most heavily weighted issues F and E with `valueC`, and compromise slightly on issue D with `valueB`.

The opponent has rigidly bid `valueA` for every issue throughout the entire negotiation, signaling extreme preference for those outcomes or employing a hardline tactic. At `t=0.62`, I must continue proposing tradeoffs that maintain my utility above 0.70 while giving the opponent `valueA` on isolated issues to map their actual priorities. I am now offering `valueA` on my lower-weight issues (A, B, and E) while securing my most crucial issues (F, C) with `valueC`.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, repeating `valueA` across all issues since the very beginning. To make a push for an agreement given the approaching deadline, I will systematically concede to `valueA` on a significant number of my lower-priority issues (A, B, and E) while maintaining a moderate utility on issues C and D with `valueB`. I am holding firm on issueF as it provides the greatest value to me.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer of `valueA` for all issues since the start of the negotiation, showing no willingness to concede. With time passing 0.66, I need to continue testing whether yielding partially on my less valuable issues (A, B, D) to their preferred `valueA` will elicit any flexibility. I am retaining `valueC` on my most crucial issue (F) while compromising to `valueB` on issues C and E to maintain a utility above 0.6.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on their initial proposal of `valueA` across all issues, showing no concessions. Given that we are advancing into the late stages of negotiation (t=0.68), I need to present a significant compromise to prompt movement. I will concede fully on my lower-weighted issues (A, B, and C) to their preferred `valueA`, compromise on D and E, while firmly maintaining my highest-valued issue F at `valueC`.

The opponent has stubbornly bid 'valueA' for every issue throughout the entire negotiation without making a single concession. At t=0.70, to invite agreement before the deadline, I will offer their preferred 'valueA' on four lower and medium priority issues (A, B, C, D) while holding firm on my highest-priority issues, E and F, with 'valueC'. This proposal still provides me a reasonable utility of 0.62 while aligning with the opponent's demands on the majority of the issues.

The opponent has rigidly bid 'valueA' for all issues throughout the entire negotiation without concession. Approaching the late stage (t=0.72), I will continue maintaining my highest-weighted issue (issueF) at 'valueC' to protect my core utility, while offering 'valueA' on almost all other issues to try to meet them where they stand. By rotating which of the 0.2-weight issues I hold at 'valueC', I can sustain a utility of 0.62 while offering substantial concessions to the stubborn opponent.

The opponent has rigidly bid "valueA" for all issues across every turn. With time advancing to the late stages (t=0.74), I am continuing to search for a compromise by yielding on their preferred values for most issues while holding firm on my most important issue (issueF). I will rotate my next concession to offer "valueC" on issueD while conceding issueC back to their preferred "valueA", maintaining my utility at approximately 56% of the maximum but offering them 4 out of 6 of their demanded values.

The opponent has rigidly held to a single offer of 'valueA' for all issues despite the time advancing to t=0.76. In an attempt to break the deadlock and discover their flexibility, I will concede heavily on the lower-importance issues (giving them 'valueA' on A, B, C, and D) while offering a slight concession on issueE ('valueB'). I am holding firm on issueF ('valueC') as it carries the highest importance to me (0.3).

The time fraction is 0.78, approaching the deadline. The opponent has relentlessly offered `valueA` for all issues without any concession. To break the deadlock while protecting my most critical interest, I will concede entirely to their position on five of the six issues (issues A, B, C, D, and E), yielding them `valueA` on all of those. I will hold firm only on `issueF` at `valueC`, which guarantees me a baseline utility of 0.46.

The opponent is completely unyielding, offering `valueA` for every single issue in every round. At t=0.8, time is getting short, but there is still room before the deadline to probe for a partial concession rather than completely caving. I will propose `valueB` for my highest-weight issue (`issueF`) and `issueE` while giving them their preferred `valueA` on all other issues, seeing if they will accept a small compromise.

The opponent has stubbornly bid 'valueA' for all issues without making a single concession since the start, signaling highly firm preferences. As we enter the late stages of negotiation (t=0.82), I will concede my highest-weighted issue (issueF) to their preferred value 'valueA' to see if this finally breaks the deadlock. To maintain a functional utility for myself (~0.47), I am counter-balancing this concession by switching issues C, D, and E to 'valueB'.

The opponent has stubbornly bid `valueA` for every issue throughout the entire negotiation without any concession. Given that `time_fraction` is 0.84, I am under pressure to concede but will not immediately accept their extremely lowball offer. I will propose a bid that fully yields to their terms on almost all issues, but retains a slightly higher utility output on my most important issue, `issueF`, to see if they are willing to make a minimal compromise.

The time fraction is currently 0.86. The opponent has persistently bid "valueA" for every single issue since the beginning and has not conceded at all. Since we are approaching the deadline, I need to try different combinations of concessions to see if any partial match satisfies their highest-weighted issues while keeping my utility above 0.5. I am conceding fully to valueA on issues A, B, and C, and offering valueB on the highest importance issues D, E, and F.

The negotiation is nearing the deadline, and the opponent has finally conceded on issueD (offering valueB). I will reciprocate by adopting their concession on issueD while lowering my demands on my lower-weighted issues. I will hold firm on issueF (valueC), my most heavily weighted issue, while seeking a middle-ground compromise on issueE to maintain a reasonable utility score above my reservation value.

The opponent has remained firm on `valueA` for most issues but has recently shown willingness to concede to `valueB` on `issueD` and `issueE`. As we are at t=0.90 with the deadline imminent, I will accept their latest position on `issueD` and `issueE` (D as `valueA` and E as `valueB`), making major concessions on all issues except my highest-weighted issue, `issueF`, where I will hold out for `valueC` to maintain a viable utility above my reservation value.

The deadline is extremely close (t=0.92). The opponent has been highly stubborn, mostly demanding `valueA` across the board, but recently signaled minor flexibility on `issueD`, `issueE`, and `issueF` by offering `valueB` for them individually. To maximize my chances of reaching an agreement while protecting my highest-value issue (`issueF`), I will fully concede to `valueA` on my lowest-weight issues (`issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`), ask for their previously offered `valueB` on `issueD` and `issueE`, and insist on `valueC` for `issueF`. This yields a utility of 0.626, which remains a favorable and realistic compromise given their recent probing.

The deadline is extremely close (t=0.94), making reaching an agreement critical to avoid a score of zero. The opponent stubbornly held their initial position until very recently but has just signaled willingness to accept 'valueB' for issues D, E, and F individually or in pairs. I am combining all of their recent concessions by offering 'valueB' for D, E, and F, while fully conceding to their preferred 'valueA' on my lower-weight issues (A, B, C). This brings my utility to ~0.51, which is a fair compromise given the time pressure.

The deadline is imminent at t=0.96. The opponent has firmly held to `valueA` on issues A, B, and C throughout the entire negotiation, but has recently shown flexibility on issues D, E, and F, notably conceding `issueE: valueC` in their last offer. To reach a mutually acceptable agreement before time runs out, I will concede entirely on A, B, and C, accept their offer of `valueC` on issue E, and request modest concessions (`valueB`) on issues D and F based on their previous flexibility.

The negotiation is at `t = 0.98`, the absolute final moments before the deadline, meaning avoiding a breakdown (and a reservation value of 0) is the priority. The opponent's last offer gives me my highest utility from them so far by yielding to `valueB` on both `issueE` and `issueF` (two of my highest-weighted issues). To guarantee an agreement, I will adopt their exact latest offer to conclude the negotiation successfully.
